sentences.text: In 2000, Fatima got an order for execution.
She spoke for women’s rights, but leaders were angry.
They wanted to kill her and that made her afraid.
Fatima is a mother with three sons: Hassan, Omar, and Yusuf.
Hassan has a heart problem that is very rare.
Only doctors in the US and UK can save his life.
The family must leave right now.
They must escape together or they will all die.
Fatima and Ahmed gathered their three boys, Omar, Hassan, and Yusuf.
They took only their clothes and some important papers.
They left their home and friends and everything they knew.
They did not know when they would come back.
They did not know if they would return one day.
They knew they must go now and leave.
Hassan's life depends on this important thing.
The journey takes eighteen months to finish.
They traveled through eight countries and saw many new things.
They went through Moscow and then Ukraine and Poland.
They slept in barns with animals in Poland.
They had no money and felt very sad.
They had no food and felt very hungry.
They were hungry, tired, and scared at the same time.
They stayed together and were happy.
The family stays together and loves each other.
Omar is ten years old when they leave.
From that moment, he was not a child anymore.
He has to be strong and brave.
He helps his parents and works hard every day.
He helps his brothers when they need him.
Hassan was sick but tried to make everyone laugh.
He played the clown and made everyone laugh.
He tells jokes and makes people laugh.
He tried to make everyone forget their bad situation.
Omar learned how to join in with others.
They all learned to laugh when they felt afraid.
They traveled through Austria, Germany, Belgium, and Holland.
Every day was dangerous and full of problems.
Every day is uncertain and brings new challenges.
They did not know where to sleep that night.
They did not know where to find food and felt worried.
They walked for many hours and felt very tired.
They ride in buses that are very crowded.
They hid in the trucks and waited quietly.
They sleep in train stations and find a place to rest.
They slept on the ground and felt cold at night.
They slept in safe places when they could find them.
They were robbed two times.
Men came at night and they were quiet.
They took the money from the family.
They took their few things and left quickly.
They took all the things the family saved and worked for.
After the robberies, the family has nothing now.
They had no food for many days and felt very hungry.
They were hungry and wanted to eat food.
They were thirsty and wanted to drink water.
They are very tired after a long day.
They did not sleep much at all.
They were afraid to sleep because of the dark.
They kept going and did not stop.
They had to keep going and not stop.
Hassan needs a doctor because he feels very sick.
The family needs safety and wants to feel safe.
They cannot stop and they feel tired.
They faced new challenges in each country they visited.
They do not speak the language and feel confused.
They did not understand the customs and felt confused.
They did not know who to trust and felt confused.
They were afraid and felt scared all the time.
They always watch and see everything around them.
They are always ready to run and help each other.
They stayed together and were happy.
Ahmed and Fatima protect their children.
The children protect each other and stay safe together.
The family is their only safety and support.
They reached Calais, a city in France, at last.
Many refugees waited in a place called the jungle.
It was a place that was very dangerous.
They must go through it to reach the United Kingdom.
Hassan was very sick at that time.
He cannot walk because he is hurt.
Ahmed's father carries him when he is tired.
Ahmed carries Hassan on his back to the lorry.
Time is short and we must hurry.
Hassan needed a doctor quickly because he could die.
They hid in the lorry and went to the UK.
When they came, volunteers waited for them.
Omar saw real smiles on people's faces and felt happy.
These people want to help others and make them happy.
They wanted to help the family feel welcome and happy.
Omar felt happy because he finally felt at home.
The family goes to the hospital for help.
Doctors check Hassan.
They helped him with care and kindness.
Hassan is fourteen years old.
He cannot speak English but wants to learn.
But the doctors did not care about Omar and Fatima.
They helped him even when it was hard.
They saved his life and felt happy.
The family was very thankful and happy.
They traveled for eighteen months and saw many places.
They lost everything and felt very sad.
Hassan is alive, but he feels very weak.
The family is together and happy.
That is what is important to us.
The family starts their new life in the UK.
It was hard and not easy for everyone.
They get a small apartment and feel happy.
They get some clothes and feel happy.
They got help to find a school for the children.
Everything feels strange and different for Omar and Fatima.
Everything feels different now for Omar and Fatima.
The weather is cold and rainy today.
The food was different and tasted strange.
The language is different from what Omar knows.
The people are different, like Omar and Fatima.
Omar is twelve years old.
He has to start a new school this year.
He has to learn a new language now.
He learns about a new country and its people.
On his first day of school, he felt very scared.
He could not understand what anyone said.
He cannot read the books and feels sad.
He could not do the homework and felt bad.
The other children look at him with surprise.
Some children are kind and help others.
Some children were not happy and played alone.
Omar feels alone and wants a friend.
He feels lost and does not know what to do.
He felt alone and thought he did not belong.
He struggles every day and feels very tired.
He felt confused and did not understand.
He felt scared and did not want to be alone.
But he did not show his feelings.
He tried to be strong but felt weak.
He tried to be brave but felt scared.
He screams inside because he feels very scared.
He shouts loudly inside and feels very strong emotions.
He asks for help inside the room.
But outside, he is quiet.
He is defensive and does not want to talk.
He tried to keep himself safe and protect himself.
He tried to hide his fear but felt scared.
He tried to hide his confusion but felt lost.
He did not want anyone to see he was scared.
Yusuf is younger than the others.
He was eight years old when they came.
He adapted quickly and learned new things fast.
He learned English quickly and spoke well.
He makes friends easily and feels happy.
But he struggled too.
He misses his old friends and feels sad.
He misses his old home and feels sad.
He misses his old life and feels sad.
Sometimes, he cries at night when he feels sad.
Fatima holds him.
She tells him that everything is okay.
She tells him that they are together.
That is what is important to us.
The family worked hard and learned English together.
They went to language classes and learned new words.
They practiced at home and learned new things.
They watched television and enjoyed the show together.
They read books and enjoy stories together.
They talked to their neighbors and shared stories.
It was hard and I felt tired.
When you cannot speak well, how can you dream big?
How do you dream and what do you see?
How can you have hope and feel good?
They kept trying and did not give up.
They kept learning and wanted to know more.
They practiced a lot and got better each time.
Ahmed finds work and is happy.
It was not the same work he did before.
It was difficult work and took a lot of time.
It was hard work and needed a lot of strength.
He was thankful and felt good.
He is grateful because he has a job.
He was happy to provide for his family.
Fatima found work and is happy about it.
She worked part-time and was home when the kids came back.
She wanted to know if they were safe.
She wanted to check if they were okay.
The family worked hard and wanted to understand the new culture.
They learned about holidays and had fun together.
They learned about customs and shared their ideas.
They learned how people lived and what they did.
They learned how people work and what they do.
They learned how people played games and had fun.
Everything is new and exciting for Omar and Fatima.
Everything feels different now for Omar and Fatima.
They learn and they were happy.
They adapt to new things and learn quickly.
They build a new life and feel happy.
It was hard for Omar and Fatima.
They worked together and finished it.
They help and support each other all the time.
They helped each other and felt happy.
When one person struggles, the others help him or her.
When one person is sad, the others comfort him or her.
When one person is afraid, the others give courage.
They stayed together as a family and loved each other.
That was their power and made them strong.
That was the hope of Omar and Fatima.
Years passed and time changed many things.
Hassan grew stronger and felt happy.
The doctors help him and continue to care for him.
They gave him medicine and he felt better.
They check his heart often and want him to be healthy.
They checked that he was healthy and strong.
Hassan is thankful for what he has.
The family is grateful for all the help they get.
The doctors save Hassan's life and he feels happy.
They gave the family hope and made them feel better.
They gave the family hope and a better future.
The family builds their new life together and feels happy.
Ahmed found a better job and feels happy.
Fatima finds better work and feels happy.
They saved money and felt happy about it.
They buy a car and feel happy.
They moved to a better apartment and feel happy.
They made friends and had fun together.
They learned the language and spoke it well.
They became part of the community and made new friends.
They go to a local mosque and join it.
They met other families and made new friends.
They celebrate holidays together and have fun.
They helped each other and felt strong together.
Omar goes to school every day and learns new things.
He worked hard and tried to do his best.
He studied hard and wanted to do well.
He learned English and now speaks it well.
He learned math and he is happy.
He learned science and enjoyed it very much.
He learned history and liked it very much.
He made friends and felt happy.
He plays football and has fun.
He joined clubs and made new friends.
He is a good student now.
He is a good friend now.
He is part of the school community now.
They never forgot where they come from and who they are.
They never forgot why they must leave.
They always remember the journey they took together.
They always remember the fear they had.
They always remember the hunger they felt.
They never forget how tired they feel.
They always remember the kindness of the people who helped them.
They never forget the helpers who greet them.
They never forget the doctors who saved Hassan's life.
They never forgot the teachers who helped Omar.
They never forget the neighbors who are friends.
They were thankful and felt happy.
They are always thankful for what they have.
Omar often thinks about his journey and feels happy.
He thinks about how his life changes now.
When he left his country, he was still Omar.
People called him a different name for some time.
People called him "a refugee" and took away his identity.
But he was still Omar and that did not change.
He was still a man and had feelings.
He is still part of a family that loves him.
The family's story is more than just a refugee story.
It is a story about a family.
It is a story about love and people.
It is a story about parents who love their children very much.
It is a story about brothers who help each other.
It is a story about hope and dreams.
It is a story about survival and hope.
It is a story about Omar and Fatima building a new life.
Omar learned he did not choose to be a refugee.
It was a gift that someone gave to him.
It was the last choice for us.
His family had to do this to be safe.
They had to do this to save Hassan's life.
They had to do this to stay alive.
Omar does not want his story to be sad.
He did not want it to be a sad story.
He wanted it to be a story of hope and joy.
He wanted a story that shows families can stay strong together.
He wanted a story that shows how kindness can change lives.
He wanted a story that shows how people can rebuild their lives.
The family lives in the United Kingdom and feels happy.
They work hard and do not stop.
They support each other and stay strong together.
They are grateful for the help they get.
They are thankful that they are together.
They are grateful that Hassan is alive.
Omar grew up and became a strong man.
He learned English and spoke it well.
He goes to school every day.
He made friends and felt happy.
He became a part of the community and made friends.
But he always remembers his journey.
He never forgets the people who help him.
He always remembers that family is very important.
He always remembers that hope is very important.
The family's story shows that refugees are real people and not numbers.
They are people and not just numbers.
They are people and they live together.
They are families and they love each other.
They are people with names and stories who leave their homes.
They are people who want safety and a better life for their children.
Omar's family lost everything and felt very sad.
But they also got something good.
They got a new home and felt happy.
They made new friends and felt happy.
They gained a new community and felt happy.
They gained each other and that was very important.
They survived together and stayed strong.
They rebuilt the house together and felt happy.
They stayed together for a long time.
That was all there was to say.
